model_narrative: This is a planning model for an integrated Mexican steel industry made up of mines, raw-material plants, and steel mills serving a set of domestic market areas. We decide how intensively to run each production process at every mine, raw-material plant, and mill; how much of each commodity to ship along every leg of the network from mines to plants to mills to markets, including interplant transfers; how much of each input to buy domestically or import; and how much finished product to export or bring in from abroad. The goal is to minimize the total cost of the whole system, which combines recurrent operating and purchasing costs, transport costs across the network, and the net cost of imports against export revenue converted at the shadow exchange rate.

model_narrative: This is a planning model for an integrated Mexican steel industry made up of mines, raw-material plants, and steel mills serving a set of domestic market areas. We decide how intensively to run each production process at every mine, raw-material plant, and mill; how much of each commodity to ship along every leg of the network from mines to plants to mills to markets, including interplant transfers; how much of each input to buy domestically or import; and how much finished product to export or bring in from abroad. The goal is to minimize the total cost of the whole system, which combines recurrent operating and purchasing costs, transport costs across the network, and the net cost of imports against export revenue converted at the shadow exchange rate.

model_narrative: This is a planning model for an integrated Mexican steel industry made up of mines, raw-material plants, and steel mills serving a set of domestic market areas. We decide how intensively to run each production process at every mine, raw-material plant, and mill; how much of each commodity to ship along every leg of the network from mines to plants to mills to markets, including interplant transfers; how much of each input to buy domestically or import; and how much finished product to export or bring in from abroad. The goal is to minimize the total cost of the whole system, which combines recurrent operating and purchasing costs, transport costs across the network, and the net cost of imports against export revenue converted at the shadow exchange rate.

sets.name: im
sets.doc: iron-ore and coal mining locations

sets.name: ir
sets.doc: raw-material plants that turn mined inputs into intermediate raw materials such as pellets and coke

sets.name: iss
sets.doc: integrated steel mills, the main production sites

sets.name: j
sets.doc: domestic market areas where finished steel is demanded

sets.name: pm
sets.doc: production processes available at the mines

sets.name: pr
sets.doc: production processes available at the raw-material plants

sets.name: ps
sets.doc: production processes available at the steel mills

sets.name: cm
sets.doc: commodities handled at the mines

sets.name: cr
sets.doc: commodities handled at the raw-material plants

sets.name: cs
sets.doc: commodities handled at the steel mills, spanning ores, intermediates, finished products and purchased inputs

sets.name: cf
sets.doc: final finished steel products sold to markets

sets.name: ce
sets.doc: commodities that may be exported

sets.name: o
sets.doc: ownership shares in the Pena Colorada pellet mine, one entry per owner

sets.name: mm
sets.doc: productive units (equipment groups) at the mines

sets.name: mr
sets.doc: productive units at the raw-material plants

sets.name: ms
sets.doc: productive units at the steel mills

params.name: km
params.doc: available capacity of each productive unit at each mine, in thousands of tons per year

params.name: kr
params.doc: available capacity of each productive unit at each raw-material plant, in thousands of tons per year

params.name: ks
params.doc: available capacity of each productive unit at each steel mill, in thousands of tons per year

params.name: d
params.doc: required deliveries of each final product to each market area, in thousands of tons per year

params.name: emax
params.doc: maximum allowed exports of each final product, in thousands of tons per year

params.name: etot
params.doc: overall ceiling on total exports across all products, in thousands of tons per year

params.name: sh
params.doc: shadow exchange rate used to convert foreign-currency import and export flows into the cost accounting

vars.name: zm
vars.doc: operating level of each mining process at each mine

vars.name: zr
vars.doc: operating level of each process at each raw-material plant

vars.name: zs
vars.doc: operating level of each process at each steel mill

vars.name: xm
vars.doc: shipments of mined commodities from a mine to a downstream raw-material plant or steel mill; only physically allowed origin-destination combinations are present

vars.name: xr
vars.doc: shipments of commodities from a raw-material plant to a steel mill

vars.name: xs
vars.doc: interplant shipments of commodities from one steel mill to another

vars.name: xf
vars.doc: shipments of finished products from a steel mill to a market area

vars.name: ur
vars.doc: domestic purchases of input commodities made at the raw-material plants

vars.name: us
vars.doc: domestic purchases of input commodities made at the steel mills

vars.name: e
vars.doc: exports of finished products shipped abroad from a steel mill

vars.name: vs
vars.doc: imports of input commodities delivered to a steel mill

vars.name: vf
vars.doc: imports of finished products delivered directly to a market area to help meet demand

vars.name: cost
vars.doc: total system cost, in millions of US dollars; the quantity being minimized

vars.name: recurrent
vars.doc: recurrent operating cost: process operating costs plus the cost of domestically purchased inputs, in millions of US dollars

vars.name: transport
vars.doc: total transport cost over every shipment leg in the network, in millions of US dollars

vars.name: imp
vars.doc: total cost of imported commodities and finished products, in millions of US dollars

vars.name: exp
vars.doc: total revenue earned from exports, in millions of US dollars

description: Exports of each product are limited. For each final product, the total amount exported across all mills must not exceed the export limit set for that product.

model_narrative: This is a planning model for an integrated Mexican steel industry made up of mines, raw-material plants, and steel mills serving a set of domestic market areas. We decide how intensively to run each production process at every mine, raw-material plant, and mill; how much of each commodity to ship along every leg of the network from mines to plants to mills to markets, including interplant transfers; how much of each input to buy domestically or import; and how much finished product to export or bring in from abroad. The goal is to minimize the total cost of the whole system, which combines recurrent operating and purchasing costs, transport costs across the network, and the net cost of imports against export revenue converted at the shadow exchange rate.

model_narrative: This is a planning model for an integrated Mexican steel industry made up of mines, raw-material plants, and steel mills serving a set of domestic market areas. We decide how intensively to run each production process at every mine, raw-material plant, and mill; how much of each commodity to ship along every leg of the network from mines to plants to mills to markets, including interplant transfers; how much of each input to buy domestically or import; and how much finished product to export or bring in from abroad. The goal is to minimize the total cost of the whole system, which combines recurrent operating and purchasing costs, transport costs across the network, and the net cost of imports against export revenue converted at the shadow exchange rate.

description: To build the complete model, enforce the following relationships in order. First, at each mine make sure each commodity produced is enough to cover everything shipped out of that mine. Second, at each raw-material plant make sure each commodity produced, received from the mines, and bought domestically covers everything shipped onward to the mills. Third, at each steel mill make sure each commodity made on site together with everything received, purchased, and imported covers everything sent to other mills, delivered to markets, and exported. Fourth, keep the use of every productive unit at each mine within its capacity. Fifth, keep the use of every productive unit at each raw-material plant within its capacity. Sixth, keep the use of every productive unit at each steel mill within its capacity. Seventh, meet every market's requirement for each final product from mill deliveries plus direct imports. Eighth, hold exports of each product within its product-specific export limit. Ninth, hold total exports across all products and mills within the overall export ceiling. Tenth, limit each owner's pellet take from the Pena Colorada mine to its ownership share of that mine's pellet capacity. Eleventh, force pellet shipments out of Alzada to be zero. Twelfth, set total cost equal to recurrent cost plus transport cost plus the net of imports minus exports converted at the shadow exchange rate. Thirteenth, set recurrent cost equal to the operating cost of the mining processes plus the cost of all domestically purchased inputs. Fourteenth, set transport cost equal to the sum over every shipment leg of its per-unit transport cost times the amount shipped. Fifteenth, set import cost equal to the sum over all imports of import price times quantity. Finally, set export revenue equal to the sum over all exports of export price times quantity.
